title: Tell me about yourself — walk me through your background and resume.

transcript.text: Tell me about yourself — walk me through your background and resume.

blocks.md:
## "Tell me about yourself / walk me through your resume" — generic strong-tech-company HM round
No company named, so I'm using a neutral hiring-manager lens (collaboration + impact + ownership). Career-arc shape, not STAR. ~150s.

> 💬 If they name a company, retune the last line to "why *them*" — say so and I'll adapt.

### The arc to hit
- **Now:** backend engineer at Walmart Data Ventures, ~2 years, supplier-facing data APIs at scale.
- **Before:** Good Creator Co. — sole architect of 6 production services (this is your range/ownership proof).
- **Start:** PayU loan disbursal — first job, real customer money, learned reliability the hard way.
- **Close:** why you're excited about this role.

---

🎙️ **Say-it script** (~150 words, read cold):

> Sure. So I'm a backend engineer, about three and a half years in, currently at Walmart Data Ventures. I build the supplier-facing data APIs — the stuff Pepsi, Coca-Cola, those accounts use to pull their Walmart sales and inventory data. Spring Boot, Java 17, Kafka, big scale, 99.9% SLA.
>
> Before Walmart I was at a startup, Good Creator Co. That's where I really grew up as an engineer — I was basically the sole architect for six production services. The one I'm proudest of: we moved our analytics off Postgres onto ClickHouse and cut query time from 30 seconds to 12, with about 30% lower infra cost.
>
> And I started at PayU, on loan disbursal. First job, real customer money moving — I got the partner API failure rate from 4.6% down to under 0.3%.
>
> So my thing is high-throughput backend systems. That's what I want to keep doing at a bigger scale, which is why I'm here.

💬 **15-second version:**
> Backend engineer, ~3.5 years. Right now at Walmart building supplier data APIs at scale. Before that I was sole architect of six services at a startup. My strength is high-throughput backend systems, and I want to do that at bigger scale — that's why I'm here.

---

**Likely follow-ups:**
- *"Why are you leaving Walmart?"* → Positive only: "I want platform-level impact at higher scale and more ownership." Never bad-mouth Walmart.
- *"Tell me more about the ClickHouse migration."* → Drop into G1: RabbitMQ buffered sinker, batch 1000 / 5s flush, MergeTree, 2-week dual-write, zero data loss.
- *"What does your current team actually do?"* → Supplier-facing data APIs for large CPG accounts; Kafka audit pipeline, 2M+ events/day.
- *"Six services solo — really?"* → Yes, ~5-person team, ~60K LOC Go + Python; name them, then go deep on one.

**What NOT to say:**
- Don't list every tech on your resume — pick the 3-job arc and one hero number per job.
- Don't recite the project bank like bullet points; it should sound like a story.
- Don't go negative on any past employer, even in passing.
- Don't run past ~2 minutes — leave room for them to dig in.

Want me to tailor this to a specific company (Amazon LP framing, Google, DigitalOcean, or your Uber SDE-2 prep)? Just name it.
